svg.title: Similarity-based Learning: Nearest-Neighbor Learning and Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor Algorithm

section.div: Nearest-neighbor learning is a type of supervised learning algorithm where the target variable is predicted by finding the class label of the most similar instance in the training data.

div.p: The algorithm is trained on a dataset of labeled instances.
When a new, unseen instance is presented, the algorithm computes the distance between this instance and all the instances in the training dataset.

section.div: 1. The algorithm is trained on a dataset of labeled instances.

div.p: 2. When a new, unseen instance is presented, the algorithm computes the distance between this instance and all the instances in the training dataset.
3. The instance with the smallest distance is selected as the nearest neighbor.
The class label of the nearest neighbor is assigned as the prediction for the new instance.

section.div: Suppose we have a dataset of labeled iris flowers, and we want to predict the species of a new, unseen flower.

div.p: The nearest-neighbor algorithm would compute the distance between this flower and all the instances in the training dataset.
The instance with the smallest distance is selected as the nearest neighbor.
The class label of this nearest neighbor would be assigned as the prediction for the new flower.

section.div: Here's an example of how you can implement the nearest-neighbor algorithm in Python:

div.pre:
import numpy as np
# Define the dataset X = np.array([[1, 2], [3, 4], [5, 6], [7, 8]]) y = np.array([0, 0, 1, 1])
# Define a function to compute the distance between two instances def distance(x1, x2): return np.linalg.norm(x1 - x2)
# Define a function to find the nearest neighbor def nearest_neighbor(x): d = [distance(x, xi) for xi in X] idx = np.argmin(d) return y[idx]
# Compute the prediction for a new instance new_x = np.array([9, 10]) prediction = nearest_neighbor(new_x)

footer.h2: Similarity-based Learning: Nearest-Neighbor Learning and Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor Algorithm
